title: MediaTek Set to Raise Chip Prices 10–20% as Supply Costs Mount; Company Wins Google TPU v9 Orders on the Back of SerDes Advantage
gist: MediaTek is raising prices 10-20% across mobile SoCs and PMICs citing component shortages and rising input costs, while winning major Google TPU v9 orders by enabling a 336G SerDes interconnect after Google's 448G target hit signal-integrity and thermal problems, displacing front-runner Broadcom. MediaTek's AI ASIC revenue is forecast at ~$2B for 2026, scaling to several billion in 2027.

insights.claim: MediaTek formally notified customers via a letter signed by COO Joe Chen that it can no longer absorb escalating input costs, citing component shortages, constrained capacity, extended supplier lead times, and rising raw material and logistics expenses.
insights.speaker: B
insights.so_what: Confirms persistent semiconductor cost inflation passing through to OEMs; margin and ASP signal across the supply chain.
insights.locator: As supply-chain cost pressures continue to build

insights.claim: MediaTek price increases on mobile SoCs and PMICs are expected to range from 10% to 15%, with cumulative increases reaching as high as 20% on certain product categories, in line with recent actions by memory makers and leading CPU vendors.
insights.speaker: B
insights.so_what: Industry-wide pricing power signal; smartphone BOM costs rising, pressuring device OEM margins.
insights.quote: increases expected to range from 10% to 15%
insights.locator: Supply chain sources indicate that the price adjustments

insights.claim: MediaTek won major orders for Google's TPU v9 by enabling adoption of a proprietary 336G SerDes interconnect solution.
insights.speaker: B
insights.so_what: Establishes MediaTek as a credible custom-AI-silicon partner to a top hyperscaler; meaningful new revenue stream.
insights.quote: MediaTek has won major orders for Google's TPU v9 by successfully enabling the adoption of a proprietary 336G SerDes interconnect solution.
insights.locator: MediaTek has reportedly secured a pivotal role

insights.claim: Google originally targeted a 448G SerDes for TPU v9 but it hit persistent signal integrity, power, and thermal challenges, and front-runner Broadcom lost its leading position due to technology maturity and timeline pressures.
insights.speaker: B
insights.so_what: Broadcom losing a TPU v9 SerDes slot is a competitive negative; watch Broadcom AI ASIC guidance and timelines.
insights.quote: Broadcom, which had been the front-runner with its own 448G SerDes development, is said to have lost its leading position for now
insights.locator: Google had originally targeted a 448G SerDes architecture

insights.claim: The DSPs needed to support 448G optical modules may require a 2nm process node, with meaningful production capacity unlikely before 2028-2029.
insights.speaker: B
insights.so_what: 448G interconnect adoption is gated to late decade, extending the window for 336G solutions like MediaTek's.
insights.quote: meaningful production capacity unlikely to materialize before 2028–2029.
insights.locator: Industry analysts further note that the digital signal processors

insights.claim: Analysts raised forecasts for MediaTek's AI accelerator ASIC revenue to approximately US$2 billion for 2026, projected to scale to several billion dollars in 2027 as AI infrastructure deployments expand, with broader traction expected among cloud service providers.
insights.speaker: B
insights.so_what: AI ASIC is becoming a material new growth vector for MediaTek beyond mobile; track CSP design-win cadence.
insights.quote: MediaTek's AI accelerator ASIC revenue to approximately US$2 billion for 2026
